g.text: Interleaved 1F1B Schedule: Pipeline Parallelism (PP) & Virtual Stages (VP)

div.p: Key Insight: Each physical GPU holds non-contiguous "virtual stages" to reduce the pipeline bubble.

ul.li: GPU 3: Virtual Stages 3, 7, 11, 15 Benefit: 4× more pipeline stages (V=4) results in smaller execution bubbles.

div.p: Overhead Trade-off: Standard PP: (P-1) communication events per micro-batch. Interleaved: (V × P) - 1 communication events. For Physical P=4, Virtual V=4:

ul.li: Interleaved: 15 P2P Transfers Verdict: Increased bandwidth requirements, but significantly reduced bubble latency.

div.p: Conclusion: Virtual stages increase effective pipeline depth, scaling bubble size by 1/V. Memory Constraint: Each GPU stores V× more activation checkpoints, though each stage compute is 1/V the size.
